violations.description: Food supplies. — --Food item was adulterated, contaminated or unsafe for human consumption. -Au Jus in refrigerator found moldy. -Mango salsa with expiration date in 2024 (open for unknown amount of time). *Disposed of immediately.
Food source requirements. — --Cooling ice was used as a food. -Diet coke can and 3 drink mixers stored directly in drink ice basin.

Preventing contamination during storage. — --Food item was not stored in a clean, dry location a minimum of 6 inches above the floor. -Multiple items stored on floor in walk in cooler: Box of Deli Express and containers of commercial salads.

Preventing contamination from equipment, utensils, and wiping cloths. — --Cloth for wiping food spills was not stored in sanitizing solution (at proper concentration) between uses. -Sani Bucket not made/available for use. *Owner stated he usually just uses a cloth with sani sink water.--Cloth or sanitizer solution for wiping food spills was not clean. -Multiple dirty cloths throughout kitchen and dish area.

Thawing potentially hazardous food. — --Potentially hazardous food was not thawed in an approved manner. -Hot dogs thawing in cold water upon arrival. *Ensure thawing is done under refrigeration, under cold running water OR as part of the cooking process.
Temperature measuring devices. — --Temperature measuring device was not provided. -No probe thermometer on-site.

Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Food contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. -Food debris a bar pizza ovens' grates.--Nonfood contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. -Debris/residue at interiors of kitchen small refrigerator and white refrigerator. -Debris at top prep surface of non-working under counter refrigerator. -Heavy grease build up surfaces of cookline equipment. -Debris/residue on condiment/water bottles used at cook line. -Frost build up at freezer interiors. -Spill at interior of table top bar refrigerator.--Ventilation hood system was not cleaned properly and/or as frequently as required. -Grease build up at hood vents.
Water Supply. — --Nonpublic water supply was not tested by EPA-certified laboratory monthly for bacteriological analysis. -Last sample conducted in August 2025.
Design and construction of floors, walls, and ceilings. — --Propane tanks (3 observed) stored in kitchen side of building. *Should not be stored indoors.

Cleaning and maintenance of floors, walls and ceilings. — --Physical facilities (e.g. floors, walls, ceiling etc. …) were not clean or not cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. -Worn/damaged floor at cook line.--Physical facilities (e.g. floors, walls, ceiling etc. …) were not maintained in good repair. -Kitchen floor due for deep clean. -Walk in cooler in need of deep clean.

Food display and service. — --Potentially hazardous food was not kept at an internal temperature of 140F or above during display or service. -Hamburgers found at 90-100F at flat top and in crock pot. Owner had removed from heat ~1 hour ago before moving majority of patties to crock pot. *Directed to reheat all patties and broth to 165F before returning to hot hold at 140F or higher. (Verified before departure-168F.)
